# io_genotypes.Variants { #tspaint.io_genotypes.Variants }

```python
tspaint.io_genotypes.Variants(
    positions,
    genotypes,
    alleles,
    sequence_length,
    sample_names=None,
    ploidy=1,
    missing=None,
    sample_index=None,
)
```

A tool-agnostic biallelic variant matrix (one column per **haplotype**).

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**positions**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(S,)`` integer site positions (sorted, strictly increasing).

<code>[**genotypes**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   ``(S, H)`` allele indices (``int8``; 0 = ancestral/REF, 1 = derived/ALT) over ``H`` haplotype columns (``samples × ploidy``).

<code>[**alleles**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[str](`str`), [str](`str`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Per-site ``(ref, alt)`` allele strings.

<code>[**sequence_length**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sequence length for the inferred tree sequence.

<code>[**sample_names**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[list](`list`)\[[str](`str`)\] or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Optional haplotype/sample names (length ``H``). For ``ploidy > 1`` these are the flattened per-haplotype names ``"<sample>_<k>"`` (0-based ``k``), as produced by the readers below.

<code>[**ploidy**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Haplotypes per sample (``H = num_samples * ploidy``); ``1`` for haploid data. Lets the front ends recover the base sample id from :attr:`sample_names` when stamping the tree sequence (:func:`tspaint.ids.attach_sample_ids`).

<code>[**missing**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Optional ``(S, H)`` boolean mask, ``True`` where the genotype was **missing** (``.`` in a VCF, negative in a Zarr) — in :attr:`genotypes` a missing call is stored as ``0`` (REF), so this mask is the only record of it. ``None`` when there is no missing data. Used by :func:`estimate_ne` to compare only the sites called in both haplotypes.

<code>[**sample_index**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[numpy](`numpy`).[ndarray](`numpy.ndarray`) or None]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Optional ``(H,)`` integer array mapping each haplotype column to the **base sample / individual** it belongs to. ``None`` means uniform ploidy — column ``j`` belongs to individual ``j // ploidy`` — which is the common case. It is set only when the samples have **mixed ploidy** (e.g. a chrX VCF with diploid females and haploid males), where a scalar ``ploidy`` cannot express the grouping; :func:`estimate_ne` and the id helpers use it to keep individuals correct.
